title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 14, 2026
headline: Nine new conduct categories in a single cycle — the methodology's most productive documentation night. Senegal's medical-science-denial enforcement and South Sudan's aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding are the most novel. Four downgrades proposed (India, Senegal, DRC, Nigeria). Six floor entities confirm at 0.0. Two boundary cases documented. xAI's third distinct Grok governance failure in five months now on record.
summary: May 14 produced the richest single-night methodology harvest in the benchmark's history: nine new conduct categories documented across fourteen assessments, including the first 'medical-science-denial enforcement' designation (Senegal charging patients with undetectable viral loads for 'voluntary HIV transmission'), the first 'aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding' (South Sudan's famine trajectory amplified by US aid cuts), and the first instance of a partial external-accountability-reversal sub-anchor positive in an AI Labs floor entity (xAI's GitHub system-prompt transparency pledge). Four downgrades are proposed: India (34.4 → 30.5, compound legislative regression including statutory removal of 2014 NALSA rights), Senegal (37.5 → 33.6, a second consecutive cycle of downward movement), DRC (4.4 → 2.3, Amnesty's 71-interview primary-source report on ADF war crimes), and Nigeria (23.4 → 21.9, a boundary case at 0.9 above the Critical threshold). Russia completed a five-phase bad-faith ceasefire-format compound — the most architecturally dense conduct documentation in the floor cluster — with 1,560+ drones deployed in 24 hours on May 14. Hungary's band-crossing proposal (Developing → Functional, 37.5 → 41.4) from May 13 remains pending carry-forward; today's EU Commission team dispatch to Budapest is a procedural milestone but sub-threshold for new scoring. Five active holds proceed: Anthropic and Microsoft expire tomorrow (May 15); OpenAI hold extends through May 21 pending Musk v. Altman verdict.

pipeline.cycleType: PRIORITY + FLOOR CONDUCT DOCS + ROTATION CARRY-FORWARD

topSignals.title: xAI Third Governance Failure Category — Same-Day Event
topSignals.description: At 3:15 AM PST May 14, an unauthorized modification to Grok's system prompt directed politically biased responses. xAI disclosed the incident same-day and pledged to publish system prompts on GitHub. This is the third structurally distinct governance failure category in 2026: (1) CSAM/NCII generation at scale, January; (2) Musk-protection prompt manipulation, February; (3) political-bias unauthorized modification, May 14. The GitHub transparency pledge is the first partial-external-accountability-reversal sub-anchor positive in an AI Labs floor entity — methodologically novel but insufficient to move the floor.

topSignals.title: Senegal Medical-Science-Denial Enforcement — New Conduct Category
topSignals.description: Senegal is charging patients with undetectable viral loads for 'voluntary HIV transmission' — a scientifically impossible harm. 24 people charged under this framing, 70+ detained under Article 319, and a 25.6% drop in HIV treatment center attendance in one month due to chilling effects. This is the first documented case of a state enforcing a criminal charge under a scientifically-incorrect framework in the current rotation. Downgrade proposed: 37.5 → 33.6 (-3.9).

topSignals.title: Russia Five-Phase Bad-Faith Ceasefire Compound Complete
topSignals.description: May 14's 1,560+ drones in 24 hours — 670 attack drones and 56 missiles in a single overnight strike — constitute the fourth and largest phase of the post-ceasefire offensive surge. The five-phase compound (announce, prepare, strike night 1, sustain night 2, large-scale escalation day 4) is now the most architecturally complete bad-faith ceasefire-format record in the floor cluster. Floor sustained at 0.0.

topSignals.title: DRC Amnesty 71-Interview Primary Source — 'I'd Never Seen So Many Bodies'
topSignals.description: Amnesty International's May 5 report documents ADF war crimes and crimes against humanity through 71 interviews: civilian massacres, forced marriage, forced pregnancy, child recruitment, abductions. 26.5M facing acute hunger; 9M expected displaced by end-2026. The April 19 Switzerland-talks positive signal is operationally hollowed by contemporaneous ADF conduct. Downgrade proposed: 4.4 → 2.3 (-2.1).

topSignals.title: Nigeria Boundary Case — 0.9 Above Critical Threshold
topSignals.description: Nigeria's proposed score of 21.9 sits 0.9 points above the Critical band floor (21.0). The world's highest number of acutely food-insecure people (35 million) and a 26% year-over-year worsening in lean-season insecurity drive a -1.5 downgrade from 23.4. A second consecutive adverse evidence cycle would push Nigeria into the Critical band. Boundary case documented.

newConductCategories.name: compound-governance-failure-cluster-with-partial-external-accountability-reversal
newConductCategories.definition: A floor entity's third structurally distinct governance failure category combined with a same-day partial external-accountability-reversal sub-anchor positive (public disclosure of bad conduct under operational pressure with forward-looking remediation promise only). Distinguishes 'full' from 'partial' external-accountability-reversal: full requires structural remediation; partial requires only public disclosure and a remediation commitment not yet executed.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: First positive sub-anchor signal documented in an AI Labs floor entity. Does not move the entity off floor but is tracked as a methodology data-point for future partial-reversal credit calibration.

newConductCategories.name: bad-faith-ceasefire-format-compound-with-large-scale-escalation
newConductCategories.definition: A five-phase compound format: (1) announce ceasefire; (2) prepare under cover of ceasefire window; (3) strike night 1; (4) sustain night 2; (5) large-scale escalation day 4. The fifth phase — large-scale escalation — extends beyond the prior three-phase template documented May 9-13.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Most architecturally complete bad-faith ceasefire-format record in the benchmark. Template now available for future ceasefire assessment reference.

newConductCategories.name: floor-conduct-with-survival-infrastructure-targeting
newConductCategories.definition: Drone warfare targeting survival infrastructure (water wells, airports) in a single 10-day window, at national scale. Water-source targeting is an IHL-protected target class requiring isolated documentation. Airport strike constitutes national-infrastructure disruption distinct from regional civilian targeting.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Two sub-categories: (1) survival-infrastructure-targeting-at-national-scale; (2) drone-warfare-geographic-de-regionalization (spread from Kordofan/Darfur to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum).

newConductCategories.name: aid-withdrawal-induced-EMP-compounding
newConductCategories.definition: EMP-dimension failure in an assessed entity amplified by an upstream-actor decision (US aid cut) that is outside the assessed entity's control. Documents downstream harm compounding where the amplifying cause is a distinct actor's policy choice.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Open methodology question: should upstream-actor compounding be tracked in the assessed entity's record or in a cross-entity compounded-harm tracking layer? documented.

newConductCategories.name: medical-science-denial-enforcement
newConductCategories.definition: A state's use of its criminal-justice system to enforce a charge under a scientifically-incorrect framework — specifically, prosecuting individuals with undetectable viral loads (medically untransmittable) for 'voluntary HIV transmission.' Simultaneously an EMP failure (denial of medical reality of the population prosecuted), an ACC failure (criminal-justice system used to enforce incorrect charges), and a healthcare-system harm (25.6% treatment-attendance drop from chilling effects).
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: First instance of this category in the benchmark. Compound ACC+EMP failure at the intersection of criminal law and public health science.

newConductCategories.name: accountability-decay
newConductCategories.definition: Non-accountability for a prior near-floor-conduct event treated as a contemporary ACC-dimension failure. The absence of accountability in the current assessment window is itself scored as a failure, not merely noted as background context. Applied here for India's ongoing non-accountability for the May 2025 Rohingya forced-into-sea deportation event.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Formal sub-category recommendation pending. The 'accountability-decay' framing allows the benchmark to track the compounding effect of non-accountability across assessment cycles.

newConductCategories.name: stated-commitment-operational-hollowing
newConductCategories.definition: A positive scoring event from a prior assessment cycle (April 19 Switzerland talks commitment to protect civilians and ease aid) is reversed when contemporaneous primary-source evidence documents that the commitment did not translate into operational change. The hollowing is documented by the temporal overlap of the stated commitment and the adverse evidence (Amnesty 71-interview report covering the same window).
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Recurs across Myanmar (100-day peace plan) and Russia (bad-faith ceasefire format) this cycle. Recommend unified 'bad-faith-commitment' compound category for cross-cluster application.

newConductCategories.name: proactive-disclosure-under-pressure-before-legislative-compulsion
newConductCategories.definition: An institution discloses or implements a transparency reform in response to regulatory or legislative pressure, but before the compulsory mechanism is fully operationalized. Sits between full external-accountability-reversal (voluntary) and reactive compliance (compelled). Sub-threshold credit may be appropriate.er to set methodological precedent.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: The Optum Rx transparent fee-based pricing model (described as a direct response to PBM reform legislation) is the triggering case. Conservative interpretation: legislation-driven, so limited ACC credit. Permissive interpretation: ahead of compulsion, so proactive INT credit. Methodology precedent under formalization.

newConductCategories.name: coercive-diplomacy-under-ceasefire
newConductCategories.definition: A state uses the threat of military resumption as a diplomatic coercion tool during an active ceasefire, to compel a specified behavior from the opposing party. Constitutes a B1 consent failure (unilateral threat violates ceasefire consent framework) and an I1 consistency failure (ceasefire-professed posture contradicted by war-resumption threat). May 3 threat to resume war to force Hamas disarmament is the triggering case.
newConductCategories.methodologicalNote: Distinct from ceasefire-violation documentation (which tracks physical conduct during ceasefires). Coercive-diplomacy-under-ceasefire tracks the use of war-threat as a political tool during nominal ceasefire.

floorEntities.conductHighlight: Third distinct Grok governance-failure category in 2026: May 14 unauthorized system-prompt modification directing politically biased responses. Five-month pattern: CSAM/NCII (Jan), Musk-protection prompt (Feb), political-bias modification (May 14). GitHub transparency pledge credited as partial-external-accountability-reversal sub-anchor positive — first in AI Labs floor cluster. Floor-exit criteria not in trajectory.

1,560+ drones in 24 hours on May 14; 670 attack drones + 56 missiles in single overnight strike; 1 killed, 36 injured in Kyiv, 11 rescued from collapsed residential building. Five-phase bad-faith ceasefire-format compound (announce, prepare, strike, sustain, large-scale escalation) now complete — most extensive single-window conduct record in the floor cluster.

Three discrete airstrike events May 14: Bilin Township (2 killed including child, 6 injured), Mindat Chin State (11 airstrikes in 2 days, 4 killed including detainees in custody), continuing Sagaing/Tanintharyi pattern. April 21 '100-day peace plan' directly contradicted by May 6-14 strike cluster. IIMM Depayin anniversary statement adds external accountability signal.

UN OHCHR: 880+ civilians killed by drones in first 4 months of 2026. Kornoi water well strike (May 12); Khartoum International airport strike (May 4). Drone warfare now extends to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum — conflict no longer regionalized. Two new conduct categories: survival-infrastructure-targeting-at-national-scale and drone-warfare-geographic-de-regionalization.

UN ERC Fletcher warned Security Council of 'full-scale famine and collapse' risk (April 18). HRW May 6: 'Civilians starving in South Sudan's conflict areas.' Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3: 7 killed, 20 injured). US aid cuts forcing UN agency scale-backs create methodologically novel aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding.

May 3 war-resumption threat to force Hamas disarmament documented as first coercive-diplomacy-under-ceasefire event. 800+ killed since October ceasefire; 8 aid workers killed; aid trucks averaging below agreed 600/day threshold. OCHA mixed signal (famine averted, 1.1M reached monthly) attributed to international aid system, not Israeli policy — not credited. Hungary ICC reversal continues to narrow leadership travel footprint.

scoreChanges.name: India

dimensions.rationale: Transgender Amendment Bill signed March 2026 — statutory removal of Supreme Court NALSA (2014) self-identification right; permits medical data sharing with authorities. Measurable legislative-regression event on a constitutionally-anchored right.

Two compounding events: (a) 36 Rohingya survivors of May 2025 Indian Navy forced-into-sea deportation remain in Thailand with no accountability mechanism — accountability-decay category; (b) May 9 Nagpur arrest for Instagram post criticizing Operation Sindoor.
HRW April 17 documented proposed rules to expand online censorship — systems-level investment in speech-suppression infrastructure. Half-step reduction.

evidence.summary: Amnesty International March 2026: Transgender Amendment Bill signed — removes 2014 NALSA self-identification right; permits medical data sharing with authorities.
HRW April 17 2026: India proposed rules to expand online censorship targeting dissent.
Fortify Rights April 9 2026: 36 Rohingya refugees forced into sea by India in May 2025 still in Thailand with no accountability mechanism from India.
Scroll.in: how India allegedly deported 40 Rohingya refugees by forcing them into the Andaman Sea.
May 9 2026 Nagpur arrest for Instagram post criticizing Operation Sindoor.

keyEvidence: March 2026: Transgender Amendment Bill signed — removes 2014 NALSA self-identification right; allows medical data sharing with authorities.
April 9 2026: 36 Rohingya survivors of May 2025 Indian Navy forced-into-sea deportation unaccounted for (accountability-decay category).
April 17 2026: HRW documented proposed rules expanding online censorship.
May 9 2026: Nagpur arrest for Instagram post criticizing Operation Sindoor.
scoreChanges.rationale: Three-dimension downgrade (EQU -0.5, ACC -0.5, SYS -0.25) reflects four-vector compound negative evidence since May 6 baseline. EQU downgrade most defensible: statutory removal of a Supreme-Court-anchored right. ACC downgrade documents 'accountability-decay' — a new sub-category where non-accountability for a prior conduct event becomes itself an active ACC failure.
watches: Supreme Court challenges to Transgender Amendment Bill
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: Composite at 30.5 sits 9.5 points above Critical threshold and 10.5 points below Functional. Not a boundary case.

scoreChanges.name: Senegal

dimensions.rationale: Criminal charges against people with undetectable viral loads (medically untransmittable) for 'voluntary HIV transmission' demonstrate empathic disregard of medical reality of the prosecuted population. 25.6% treatment-attendance drop represents people deterred from care by state criminalization.

State response to HIV-treatment-attendance drop has not included corrective measures. 70+ arrests + 24 'transmission' charges + 6-year first conviction is disproportionate enforcement. State action is structurally causing the harm being addressed by international health institutions.
Further LGBTQ-rights regression beyond May 9 baseline. Compound differential-harm pattern targeting a specific minority population. Approaching floor (1.0).
State enforcement of medically-incorrect criminal charges is a documented accountability-mechanism failure — the criminal-justice system enforces a charge that is scientifically not transmissable.

evidence.summary: TheNewHumanitarian May 5 2026: 25.6% drop in HIV treatment center attendance in one month due to Article 319 chilling effects.
UNAIDS March 18 2026 condemnation of Senegal Article 319 law and its impact on HIV response.
HIV Justice Network: 70+ arrested under Article 319 since February; 24 charged with 'voluntary HIV transmission' including people with undetectable viral loads; first 6-year conviction April 10 2026.
The World May 5 2026: In Senegal, concerns mount over impact of anti-LGBTQ laws on HIV treatment.

keyEvidence: 25.6% HIV treatment center attendance drop in one month — new quantified harm metric.
24 charged with 'voluntary HIV transmission' including people with undetectable viral loads — medical-science-denial enforcement.
scoreChanges.rationale: Second consecutive cycle of downward movement for Senegal. The 25.6% HIV-treatment-attendance drop is the cleanest quantified-harm metric in the cycle — directly measurable downstream healthcare harm attributable to state action. The medical-science-denial enforcement sub-category (prosecuting undetectable-viral-load patients) is a novel compound ACC+EMP failure.
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: Composite at 33.6 sits 12.6 points above Critical threshold. Not a boundary case. Further downward movement in subsequent cycles could approach Critical if Article 319 enforcement intensifies.

scoreChanges.name: Democratic Republic of Congo

dimensions.rationale: Amnesty 71-interview testimony of forced marriage and forced pregnancy as a weapon documented under state non-protection. Empathic capacity to recognize and respond to these harm types not in evidence. Moved to floor.
May 3 Switzerland-talks commitment to protect civilians operationally hollowed by Amnesty May 5 documentation of contemporaneous ADF conduct. AC4 resource mobilization disrupted by OCHA funding gap.
Sexual violence as weapon (forced marriage, forced pregnancy) + child recruitment + civilian massacres + ransom abduction = comprehensive boundary-scope failure under state non-protection.
No accountability mechanism for documented ADF conduct. OCHA forced to triage response.
26.5M facing acute hunger; 9M expected displaced by end-2026; OCHA triage = systems-level humanitarian-architecture collapse.
Stated commitments (April 19 Switzerland talks) not matched by operational conduct (May 5 Amnesty). Integrity-of-promises signal weakened.

evidence.summary: Amnesty International May 5 2026: 'I'd Never Seen So Many Bodies' — 71-interview primary-source documentation of ADF war crimes and crimes against humanity.
Full Amnesty report (afr62/0860/2026): civilian massacres, abductions, forced labor, child recruitment, sexual violence including forced marriage and forced pregnancy.
Al Jazeera May 4 2026: extensive brutality — rebel attacks reap hell on Congolese civilians.
OCHA: 26.5M facing acute hunger; 9M expected displaced by end-2026; humanitarian community forced to strictly prioritize response due to funding gap.

keyEvidence: Amnesty International May 5 'I'd Never Seen So Many Bodies' — 71-interview primary-source of ADF war crimes and crimes against humanity.
Documented: civilian massacres, abductions, forced labor, child recruitment, sexual violence including forced marriage and forced pregnancy.
26.5M people facing acute hunger (OCHA); 9M expected displaced by end-2026.
April 19 Switzerland-talks positive signal operationally hollowed by Amnesty May 5 contemporaneous conduct documentation.
OCHA funding gap forces triage of humanitarian response — systems-level architecture stress.
scoreChanges.rationale: Six-dimension downgrade. The May 3 positive sub-anchor signal (April 19 Switzerland talks) is operationally hollowed by the May 5 Amnesty 71-interview report. The 'stated-commitment-operational-hollowing' conduct category is now documented in three floor-cluster entities this cycle (DRC, Myanmar 100-day peace plan, Russia ceasefire format) — a cross-cluster pattern requiring unified methodology framing.
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: Composite at 2.3 sits 18.7 points below Critical-Developing threshold (21). Not a boundary case. Floor-proximate.

scoreChanges.name: Nigeria

dimensions.rationale: 35M acutely food insecure — world's highest projected number. 5.8M in acute food insecurity during May-October lean season (26% year-over-year increase from 4.6M). State-level recognition-and-response gap of comparative historical scale.
The New Humanitarian April 27 2026: 'state dysfunction' analysis is a primary-source SYS-dimension assessment isolating governance failure as the cause of inadequate humanitarian response.

evidence.summary: UN News Jan 2026 (recirculated May 2026): Nigeria has world's highest projected number of acutely food-insecure people — nearly 35 million citizens. 5.8M facing acute food insecurity during May-October lean season (up from 4.6M, 26% YoY increase).
The New Humanitarian April 27 2026: To deliver better humanitarian response, fix Nigeria state dysfunction.
HRW World Report 2026 Nigeria chapter: Boko Haram/JAS resurgence, ISWAP operations, IPOB Imo killings, security force abuses.

keyEvidence: Nigeria has world's highest projected number of acutely food-insecure people — nearly 35 million citizens.
5.8M facing acute food insecurity during May-October lean season (26% YoY increase from 4.6M).
The New Humanitarian April 27 2026: 'state dysfunction' analysis (SYS-dimension primary source).
BOUNDARY CASE: proposed composite 21.9 sits 0.9 points above Critical threshold (21.0).
scoreChanges.rationale: Conservative two-dimension downgrade (EMP -0.25, SYS -0.25). The 'world's highest' food-insecurity designation is a new comparative-scale finding. The TNH state-dysfunction analysis is a primary-source SYS-dimension assessment. Boundary case flagged: one additional adverse evidence cycle could push Nigeria into the Critical band.
carryForward: Borno Police Commissioner May 4 anti-torture warning
scoreChanges.boundaryWatchResolution: BOUNDARY CASE — composite at 21.9 sits 0.9 points above Critical-Developing threshold (21.0). Logged per boundary-case protocol. Conservative interpretation preserves Developing-band placement. Any additional negative evidence in subsequent cycles risks Critical classification.

confirmations.watchReason: Monsoon watch day 2 of 2. PDMA/ECHO confirmed active severe weather system across multiple provinces; weather system continues through May 17; most active period May 13-15. NDMA active. No mass-casualty event in 24h window. Watch continues through May 17.
confirmations.headline: Pakistan MONSOON WATCH DAY 2 — confirmed at 20.3 Developing. No mass-casualty flooding in 24h window. PDMA/NDMA active response. Watch closes May 17 if no mass-casualty event documented from May 13-17 weather system.

confirmations.asymmetricConductNote: Russia's May 14 mass civilian strike (1,560+ drones, 1 killed/36 injured in Kyiv) and Ukraine's defensive posture strengthen the post-ceasefire asymmetric-conduct pattern. +3.1 May 11 carry-forward credit sustained. No mid-cycle expansion per carry-forward protocol.
confirmations.headline: Ukraine ASYMMETRIC-CONDUCT CONFIRMATION at 50.0 Functional. May 14 Russia mass strike demonstrates pattern asymmetry: Ukraine sustained defensive posture, targeted Russian command posts only. +3.1 post-ceasefire credit sustained. No evidence of Ukrainian-attributed large-scale civilian targeting in 24h window.

confirmations.flagReason: Mixed-signal cycle. Optum Rx transparent fee-based pricing model (May 13) is the first proactive transparency reform from UnitedHealth in the investigation window. Conservative interpretation: legislation-driven, limited ACC credit, +0.125 INT half-step only. Net +0.5 sub-threshold. Flagged to set methodological precedent for 'proactive-vs-reactive' reform classification under regulatory pressure.
confirmations.headline: UnitedHealth Group DOCUMENTED: +0.5 sub-threshold movement (10.9 → 11.4). Optum Rx transparent pricing model credited as first proactive transparency reform (+0.125 INT half-step) under new 'proactive-disclosure-under-pressure-before-legislative-compulsion' sub-category. Methodology precedent under formalization. DOJ probe and court-ordered AI disclosure offset further credit.

boundaryWatchEntities.status: BOUNDARY CASE — proposed composite 21.9 sits 0.9 points above Critical threshold (21.0). One additional adverse evidence cycle risks band crossing to Critical.
boundaryWatchEntities.watchReason: World's highest acute food insecurity (35M), 26% YoY worsening, state dysfunction analysis all documented this cycle. Lean season May-October is the immediate risk window.

boundaryWatchEntities.status: CARRY-FORWARD PENDING — May 13 band-crossing proposal (Developing → Functional, 37.5 → 41.4) remains pending. Today's EU Commission team dispatch to Budapest is sub-threshold supportive but not a new scoring trigger. Next material triggers: May 25 Brussels visit, May 31 Sulyok dismissal deadline.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Sudan-South Sudan Compound Humanitarian Crisis
sectorAlerts.alert: Sudan (880+ drone deaths Jan-Apr 2026; survival-infrastructure targeting) and South Sudan (full-scale famine warning from UN ERC; Fangak hospital strike May 3) both document escalating compound crises in adjacent territories. South Sudan's aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding introduces a novel cross-actor harm amplification vector: US aid cuts to South Sudan create downstream EMP failure in the assessed entity. Two floor entities, compound regional humanitarian collapse.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — South Asia Legislative Regression Cluster
sectorAlerts.alert: India (Transgender Amendment Bill removing NALSA rights; -3.9 proposed) and Senegal (medical-science-denial enforcement; -3.9 proposed) both show four-vector harm expansions since their most recent assessments. India's EQU regression removes a Supreme Court-anchored right via legislation. Senegal's EQU regression deepens an already-band-crossed entity. Cross-index legislative-regression cluster pattern visible.

May 14 active conduct events: Russia (1,560+ drones, 1 killed/36 injured in Kyiv), Myanmar (three discrete airstrike events, 6 killed including child), Sudan (Kornoi water well; 880+ drone deaths documented), South Sudan (hospital strike, famine warning). Four floor entities requiring concurrent conduct documentation.

No new flags added to cluster.

highlights: Tonight produced the richest single-night methodology harvest in the benchmark's history: nine new conduct categories across fourteen assessments. The medical-science-denial enforcement sub-category (Senegal charging undetectable-viral-load patients with 'voluntary HIV transmission') and the aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding (South Sudan's famine trajectory amplified by upstream US aid policy) are the most analytically novel contributions.

Russia's bad-faith ceasefire-format compound now extends to five phases — the most architecturally dense conduct documentation in the floor cluster. The May 14 1,560+ drone deployment in 24 hours is the largest single-day strike package since the ceasefire expired. The five-phase template (announce, prepare, strike, sustain, large-scale escalation) is now available as a reference framework for any future ceasefire-format assessment.

xAI's third Grok governance failure in five months establishes a systemic pattern that no single-employee root-cause attribution can explain. The partial-external-accountability-reversal sub-anchor (GitHub system-prompt transparency pledge) is the first positive signal in an AI Labs floor entity — crediting it without moving the entity off floor required a new methodology sub-distinction between 'partial' and 'full' external-accountability-reversal.

The DRC Amnesty 71-interview primary-source report is the highest-quality new evidence generated in the May 14 cycle. It reverses the April 19 Switzerland-talks credit and documents sexual violence as a weapon (forced marriage, forced pregnancy) under state non-protection. 'Stated-commitment-operational-hollowing' now appears across three floor-cluster entities this cycle (DRC, Myanmar, Russia) — a cross-cluster pattern warranting unified methodology treatment.

Nigeria's proposed 21.9 sits 0.9 points above the Critical threshold — the narrowest active boundary margin in the countries index. The world's highest acute food-insecurity count (35 million) and 26% year-over-year lean-season worsening make this a credible boundary case, not a rounding artifact. One additional adverse evidence cycle (lean season May-October) could trigger the crossing.

sectorTrends.summary: All six floor-cluster entities assessed tonight confirm at 0.0, but with significantly expanded conduct documentation. Russia's five-phase bad-faith ceasefire compound is now the benchmark's most complete documented conduct architecture. Sudan's de-regionalization of drone warfare to national infrastructure (airports, water wells) and its 880+ documented drone deaths establish the most quantitatively anchored floor record in the index. Myanmar's 100-day peace plan contradiction is methodologically parallel to Russia's bad-faith ceasefire format — two 'stated-de-escalation-rhetoric-with-enacted-escalation-conduct' compounds in the same cycle. Israel's coercive-diplomacy-under-ceasefire designation adds a new conduct sub-category to an already comprehensive floor record. South Sudan's aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding introduces an upstream-actor harm amplification vector that has no prior benchmark precedent.

India and Senegal both show four-vector harm expansions since their most recent assessments. India's regression is anchored in a statutory removal of a Supreme Court-affirmed right — the cleanest legislative-regression signal the benchmark has documented. Senegal's regression is anchored in a quantified harm metric (25.6% HIV treatment attendance drop) that translates legislative action directly into measurable healthcare-system failure. Both are Developing-band entities; neither is at immediate Critical risk; both show compound downward trajectories. The compound legislative-regression-with-enforcement cluster pattern may indicate a broader EQU-dimension weakening in upper-Critical and lower-Developing entities globally.

sectorTrends.sector: AI Labs — Governance and Regulatory Pressure
sectorTrends.summary: xAI's third governance failure category extends the benchmark's most concentrated AI governance failure record. Three structurally distinct failures in five months — CSAM/NCII generation, deliberate partisan-alignment prompt manipulation, and unauthorized employee system-prompt modification — demonstrate a systemic governance architecture problem that individual-employee attribution cannot explain. The GitHub transparency pledge generates the first partial-external-accountability-reversal sub-anchor positive in an AI Labs floor entity, opening a new methodology question about how sub-threshold positive signals should be calibrated in floor contexts. Anthropic and Microsoft hold expiries tomorrow and Google's hold expired today will add three major entities to the May 15 assessment queue — the most significant AI labs scoring window since the Pentagon cohort decision.

UnitedHealth's Optum Rx transparent pricing model is the first proactive transparency reform from the entity in the current investigation window. The benchmark's conservative interpretation (legislation-driven, not fully voluntary) limits ACC credit and produces only a sub-threshold +0.5 movement. The more significant contribution is the methodology question it surfaces: how should reform announcements that precede but are responsive to legislative pressure be classified? The Editorial review's decision on UnitedHealth will set precedent for a category of reform signals that will recur across the Fortune 500 index.

emergingRisks.description: Nigeria's proposed composite of 21.9 sits 0.9 points above the Critical band threshold (21.0). The May-October lean season is the immediate risk window: 5.8M are already in acute food insecurity at lean-season onset, up 26% year-over-year. If OCHA or UN IPC data documents deterioration in June-July, the EMP dimension will face further downward pressure on an entity already at the boundary. Nigeria entering the Critical band would be the most significant countries-index band change of the year after Hungary.

Senegal's EQU dimension is now at 1.25 — approaching the 1.0 floor. Two consecutive cycles of downward EQU movement (May 9 band crossing, May 14 further regression) establish a clear trajectory. If Article 319 enforcement continues at the current rate (70+ detained, 24 charged, first 6-year conviction) or expands to additional target populations, EQU could reach floor in the next 1-2 cycles. An EQU floor designation would trigger a comprehensive review of Senegal's band placement.

emergingRisks.risk: Open Bionics formula audit publication integrity
emergingRisks.description: Open Bionics now at 12 cycles without formula audit resolution. Published 97.5 carries a -10.0 reconstruction discrepancy. At 12 cycles this is an active publication-integrity crisis, not a maintenance item. Every cycle this remains unresolved, the benchmark publishes a score known to be materially incorrect by 10 points across one band boundary (Exemplary vs. Established).
emergingRisks.timeframe: Immediate — audit must begin this week

emergingRisks.risk: Musk v. Altman verdict cascading AI Labs scoring event
emergingRisks.description: Closing arguments begin May 14-15; advisory jury deliberates with Judge Gonzalez Rogers ruling expected mid-May. A charitable-trust-breach finding against OpenAI would be a direct ACC/SYS scoring event for OpenAI (hold expires May 21) and a contextual event for Microsoft (hold expires May 15). The trial has also produced the xAI admission of OpenAI model distillation — a potential INT/BND signal for xAI independent of the verdict. Three held AI Labs entities (Anthropic, Microsoft, OpenAI) and one expired hold (Google) create the largest single-week AI Labs scoring queue since April.

Sudan (880+ drone deaths documented; airport and water-well strikes; conflict de-regionalized to all major population centers) and South Sudan (full-scale famine warning from UN ERC; hospital strike; US aid cuts compounding EMP failure) represent a compound regional collapse trajectory. Both entities are at floor; neither has a credible floor-exit pathway. The aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding in South Sudan introduces a new risk vector: external policy decisions (US aid policy) can amplify humanitarian failure in assessed entities independently of those entities' own conduct.

insights: May 14 documented the first 'partial external-accountability-reversal' sub-anchor positive in an AI Labs floor entity (xAI's GitHub system-prompt pledge) and the first 'medical-science-denial enforcement' designation (Senegal charging undetectable-viral-load patients). Both require new methodology sub-distinctions: the first between partial and full external-accountability-reversal; the second formalizing prosecutorial science-denial as a compound ACC+EMP failure category. Nine new conduct categories in a single cycle suggests the methodology's vocabulary is expanding faster than its consolidation pace — a sign of a rich evidentiary environment but also a methodological housekeeping need.

The 'stated-commitment-operational-hollowing' pattern now recurs across three floor-cluster entities in this cycle alone: DRC (April 19 Switzerland talks vs. May 5 Amnesty report), Myanmar (April 21 100-day peace plan vs. May 6-14 strike cluster), Russia (May 9-11 ceasefire format vs. May 9-14 offensive surge). This cross-cluster frequency suggests the pattern is systematic enough to warrant a unified conduct category rather than entity-specific designations. Formalizing it as a top-level category would allow the benchmark to track the prevalence of stated-commitment gaps across the full entity set as a structural indicator of institutional integrity.
